title: Time Constant and Filtering
summary: This page explains how a system’s time constant affects its responsiveness and filtering behavior. A large time constant leads to sluggish response and suppression of rapidly varying inputs, illustrating lowpass filtering with figure examples comparing high- and low-frequency sinusoidal inputs.

figures.caption: Rise time of a system: step input x(t), rectangular h(t), and ramp-to-constant output y(t)
Time constant and filtering: rectangular h(t), convolution-window views with high- and low-frequency sinusoids, and corresponding outputs
